## [03:01 - 05:57]

Like, it should come up like, there should be like a reason why you're calling. Like, you got to close, maybe you know you had a hard call, whatever, right? But we just kind of want to be temperature checking our reps throughout the day, throughout the week. They're going to be doing call reviews. Again, there's kind of two main categories of call reviews. There's a live breakdowns. I would do three per week when you start off and then whittle down the one per week minimum. That's where you actually sit down live with the rep on Zoom, go through the recording with them. That can be on the meeting or it can be in a separate one-on-one. It's fine either way. Then there's also the temp checks, which is just not with the rep, just you know, whether you're working out, driving, throwing a call, and just listen to it at 1x, 1.5x speed, whatever you want to do, and just get kind of a good qualitative temp check on how the rep is sounding. Obviously, if you see any issues, you can step in, send them a voice message, call them, whatever, give them some advice, or don't. I mean, it just is really up to you. But this is just so you can get a good idea and a constant temp check as a manager. How people are doing on the front lines.
